19AS91274 - INVESTIGATE AN ASPECT OF NEW ZEALAND MUSIC
20How do I prepare for this assessment?
21WHAT SHOULD I INCLUDE IN MY INVESTIGATION?

What to do

You will be assessed on four different music standards:

  • Solo Performance: Perform two substantial pieces of music as a featured soloist.
  • Group Performance: Demonstrate ensemble skills by performing a substantial piece of music as a member of a group.
  • Composition: Compose two substantial pieces of music.
  • Investigate an Aspect of New Zealand Music: Investigate an aspect of New Zealand music.

Key requirements

  • Solo Performance:
  • Perform two substantial pieces as a featured soloist.
  • Pieces should be 4-5 minutes long, musically and technically complex.
  • Demonstrate technical, musical, and presentation skills.
  • Live presentation to an audience.
  • Group Performance:
  • Perform a substantial piece as a member of a group (3-7 members recommended).
  • Contribute to the group's cohesion, balance, intonation, feel, style, and accuracy.
  • Demonstrate confidence, consistency, and musical responsiveness.
  • Piece should be 4-6 minutes long, with significance and complexity.
  • Composition:
  • Compose two substantial pieces of music, each at least two minutes in length.
  • Musical ideas should be skilfully developed and structured.
  • Demonstrate understanding of idiomatic writing, effective use of scales/modes, and compositional devices.
  • Structure ideas into a coherent whole with a clear beginning, middle, and end.
  • Ability to create and maintain a sense of style.
  • Can be solo or group composition (2-5 students for group).
  • Investigate an Aspect of New Zealand Music:
  • Conduct an investigation into an aspect of New Zealand music.
  • This could be a musical style, genre, historical period, music industry, advocacy group, funding agency, musician, performing group, or festival/competition.
  • Presentation can include written assignments, seminars, documentaries, websites, PowerPoints, or static displays.

Tips

  • Music Ambassadors: Consider your skills and ideas for leading clubs or strengthening the music community.
  • Solo Performance:
  • Choose pieces that showcase your best skills and that you will enjoy practicing.
  • Practice regularly (aim for at least 20 minutes a day).
  • Practice performing in front of classmates.
  • Bring a copy of your score or a link to a performance of your piece.
  • A quality performance is assured, stylistically appropriate, musically expressive, demonstrates interpretive understanding, has appropriate body language, and is introduced by you.
  • Group Performance:
  • Make the most of class rehearsal time and organize extra rehearsals if needed.
  • Bring a copy of your score or a link to a performance of your piece.
  • Quality performance includes enthusiastic interaction, musical responsiveness, awareness of space and audience, self-assurance, fluency, and an appropriate piece.
  • Composition:
  • Engage in regular feedback and re-work your compositions.
  • Keep good records of your drafts and process.
  • Submit both visual representations (e.g., notation, lead sheet) and audio recordings.
  • For group composition, ensure your contribution can be verified (e.g., annotations, diary entries).
  • Investigate an Aspect of New Zealand Music:
  • Your investigation should include a chronological overview, background profile, significant musical characteristics, and an evaluation of significance.
  • Presentation can include elements like performance, music videos, or annotated scores.

Dates & deadlines

  • Term 1:
  • Week 1: Induction
  • Week 8: Music Camp
  • Week 9: Picnic Evening
  • Week 10: Tauranga
  • Week 11: Assessment for Investigate an Aspect of New Zealand Music (TBC)
  • Term 2:
  • Week 5: SFRQ (Heats)
  • Week 6: Manawatu
  • Week 8: Big Sing
  • Week 9: Solo 1 (June 19)
  • Week 9: SFRQ (Finals)
  • Week 10: Wind Festival
  • Week 11: Musical!
  • Term 3:
  • Week 8: DG exams
  • Week 9: Music Evening
  • Week 9: Performance 2 (after DGE)
  • Week 10: Formal feedback for Composition - Piece 2
  • Term 4:
  • Week 4: Last lesson for Y12
  • Week 5: NCEA exams
  • Week 9: Formal feedback for Composition - Piece 1
  • Week 10: Final hand-in for Composition
  • Current 2024 assessment dates (TBC and subject to change):
  • Performance 1 (Solo): Term 2 Week 9 (19 June)
  • Performance 2 (Solo): Term 3 Week 9 (after DGE)
  • Music Evenings (TBC) and External Music Festivals (Group Performance)
  • Formal feedback for Composition: Term 2 Week 10 (Piece 1), Term 3 Week 8 (Piece 2)
  • Final hand-in for Composition: Term 3 Week 10
  • Assessment date for Investigate an Aspect of New Zealand Music: Term 1 Week 11 (TBC)
